_note: The `vector` arrays below are ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDERS (8 values shown). Real vectors have 384 floats each and must be generated by your build script (see README). Do not ship these placeholders.

chunks.text: A list comprehension builds a list. The form is [expression for item in iterable if condition]. The if clause is optional and filters items.
A dict comprehension builds a dictionary with {k: v for k, v in pairs}. A set comprehension builds a set and removes duplicates.
A generator expression uses parentheses and yields items lazily one at a time instead of building the whole list in memory, which is more memory-efficient for large sequences.
Use a list comprehension when you need the full list; use a generator expression when you only pass through the data once and want to avoid holding it all in memory.
